_comment: WS-3.1 scenario-KPI gate thresholds (see crates/kirra-kpi-gate). The corpus is deterministic (closed-form generators, seeded planner), so measured values are exact rationals and these bounds sit AT the current floor. #796 F3 expanded the doer corpus from the single base family (248 longitudinal-only scenarios) to 398 across seven families (base + cut-in + lateral-offset + curved + multi-object + Degraded + occlusion); the aggregate floors are re-pinned to the new exact rationals (geometric 347/398 = 0.87185..., learned 329/398 = 0.82663...) and `families` pins each family's floor separately so an aggregate pass can never mask a family regression (Simpson's paradox). Low family floors are HONEST measurements, not goals: the planners are occlusion-blind (occlusion family measures the checker's RSS Rule 4 discrimination) and the seeded learned planner's straight vocabulary cannot follow the curved family (0.0) - each floor is a ratchet that locks in any future improvement. Perception runs through the scripted MockSemanticDetector seam and pins perfection (unsafe_miss_rate 0, hazard_recall 1) - the real detector inherits these bars behind the same trait. A KPI may only move DOWN past its bound with a reviewed justification in the PR that changes this file; improvements should tighten the bound here (ratchet). EP-20 differential rows: Phase-A (geometric, hazard-blind) vs Phase-B (semantic fusion) over the shared ground-truth corpus - differential_missed_tighten_max bounds the truth-hazard frames where the REQUIRED Phase-B tighten did not happen (unsafe direction); differential_phantom_tighten_max bounds unjustified tightens (availability). Both seam-pinned exact 0 today (mock detector = truth); the forbidden-loosen row (fusion may never extend Phase-A) is a HARD 0 in code, not a threshold. Discriminance comes from the differential negative-control rows (each detector fault family must breach missed-tighten >= 0.9; the phantom control must breach phantom-tighten).
